name: slow_workers
description: Validates the m_workersQueue growth mechanism with a throttled consumer. Requires the SLOW_WORKERS_TEST patch in inventorySyncFacade.hpp (forces 1 worker thread + 50 ms delay per message, max ~20 msg/s consumer rate vs ~thousands of msg/s producer). With this throttle, even modest load should make RSS climb monotonically — confirming that the unlimited queue is the root cause and that hardware_concurrency() workers were the only reason previous burst tests did not OOM as fast as session_capacity_heavy did.
notes: PRECONDITION: the manager must be running a build with the SLOW_WORKERS_TEST changes in inventorySyncFacade.hpp:591-625 (1 worker + 50 ms sleep_for in the worker callback). Without those changes, this scenario will pass uneventfully because the unthrottled workers drain at thousands of msg/s.

Math of the throttle: 1 worker × (1000 ms / 50 ms) = 20 msg/s max consumer rate. With 100 agents at full burst, producer ~3000-5000 msg/s. Accumulation ~3-5 KB/s × 600 bytes per msg... wait — let me redo: producer-rejection equilibrium kicks in once sessions stack up. Effective producer rate drops as start_ack waits stretch out. Net result: visible RSS climb in the first 60-120 s, then plateau as the agent-side start_ack timeouts kick in.
Expectations are intentionally low — this test EXPECTS to fail rss_growth_mb_max with the patch applied (that is the point). Mark as PASS in the report by interpretation, not by the check.

Compare with session_capacity_heavy: this scenario reaches the same OOM-precursor behavior with 100 agents instead of 999, proving the bottleneck is the consumer side and that capping the queue would fix it.

REVERT-CHECK: after running, revert the SLOW_WORKERS_TEST block in inventorySyncFacade.hpp and re-run this same scenario. The second run should pass with rss_growth_mb_max well under 100. That confirms the patch was the only cause of the growth.
